name: Sierra de Brufaganya – circular desde Querol por el santuario Sant Jaume de Montagut
region_intro: Mountain biking around Querol, located in Catalonia, Spain, offers diverse terrain within the Alt Camp region. The area is characterized by hilly landscapes, mountain passes like Coll de Coloma, and forested sections, providing varied challenges for mountain bikers. Routes often traverse through natural features of the Sierra de Brufaganya, with some extending towards the Penedès wine region. This environment provides a mix of climbs, descents, and scenic paths suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Querol The most popular mountain bike trail is Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail, a 23.1 miles (37.2 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a comprehensive experience of the Penedès region's varied landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Brufaganya – circular desde Querol por el santuario Sant Jaume de Montagut, a moderate 20.4 miles (32.9 km) path. This route explores the Sierra de Brufaganya, featuring natural scenery and a visit to Sant Jaume de Montagut. Local mountain bikers also love the Coll de Coloma Pass – Old Bridge of Montblanc loop from el Pont d'Armentera, a 67.9 miles (109.3 km) trail leading through mountain passes and historical areas, often completed in about 10 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Querol is defined by its mountain passes, hilly terrain, and forested areas. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Querol are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 29 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Querol's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Querol?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike trails around Querol, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 10 easy, 46 moderate, and 16 difficult routes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Querol?
A: Yes, Querol offers several trails suitable for beginners. You can find 10 easy routes that provide a gentle introduction to mountain biking in the area, often traversing less challenging terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Querol?
A: The terrain around Querol is diverse, characterized by hilly landscapes, mountain passes like Coll de Coloma, and forested sections. You'll encounter a mix of climbs, descents, and scenic paths, with routes often traversing the natural features of the Sierra de Brufaganya and extending towards the Penedès wine region.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Querol?
A: Yes, Querol features several circular mountain bike routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945477709/sierra-de-brufaganya-circular-desde-querol-por-el-santuario-sant-jaume-de-montagut" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Brufaganya – circular desde Querol por el santuario Sant Jaume de Montagut&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 20.4-mile (32.9 km) path that explores the Sierra de Brufaganya and visits Sant Jaume de Montagut.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails in Querol?
A: Many trails in Querol offer views of natural features and landmarks. You might encounter various peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3010235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Clapí Vell Summit (704 m)&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4392014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montagut Peak (962 m)&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by beautiful water features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3478596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Formigosa Lake&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2980038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool of the Gaia River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31569766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Coloma Pass – Old Bridge of Montblanc loop from el Pont d'Armentera&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 67.9-mile (109.3 km) trail that takes about 10 hours to complete, leading through mountain passes and historical areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Querol?
A: Querol's Mediterranean climate generally makes mountain biking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winter rides are also possible, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Querol?
A: While specific family-friendly trails aren't highlighted, the area offers 10 easy routes that could be suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. These trails typically have less elevation gain and smoother surfaces, providing a more relaxed ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Querol?
A: The mountain bike trails in Querol are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 29 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, scenic beauty, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that explore the Penedès wine region?
A: Yes, some routes extend towards the Penedès wine region, offering a unique cycling experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946107428/penedes-360-all-mountain-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penedès 360 All Mountain Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 23.1-mile (37.2 km) trail that provides a comprehensive experience of the region's varied landscape.

Q: Are there options for longer, multi-hour mountain bike tours in Querol?
A: Absolutely. For those looking for longer rides, there are several options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33821450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Coloma Pass – Old Bridge of Montblanc loop from Santes Creus&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 71.8 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31597953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Coloma Pass – Old Bridge of Montblanc loop from Monestir de Santes Creus&lt;/a&gt; covers 103.6 km, both offering extensive riding experiences.
tips: Gothic-style church located in the municipality of Querlo, in Alt Camp. The façade has two steeple bell towers and the access door is made up of three arches.
Lake Formigosa is located in the municipality of Querlo, gate of the New Houses of Formigosa and the Clota Fountain.

Sanctuary of baroque architecture located at an altitude of 760 meters, within the municipality of Pontils.
Below the sanctuary is the Chapel of the Fuentes de Sant Magí. Segundo explains the legend, the water from this fountain is considered miraculous after the saint made it sprout by hitting the ground three times with his cane at the moment when the Roman soldiers took him captive.
The tiny hamlet hidden away by the river. Make sure you check out the ancient tower and chapel on the road above.
The meanders of the river hide some natural pools, ideal for finding absolute tranquility and cooling off.

waypoints.name: Church of Sant Jaume de Montagut
